title: Hybrid Cache for Amazon S3 Cache Rules
description: Ordered list of cache rules for Hybrid Cache for Amazon S3. Place this file at cache_dir/cache_rules.json. Each rule matches a glob pattern against the full cache key {bucket}/{object_key} and supplies an optional subset of cache settings. Resolution is first-match-per-field over the ordered rule list: for each field independently, the value is taken from the earliest rule whose pattern matches and which sets that field, falling through to the global YAML cache: defaults. The file is optional and hot-reloadable; when absent or when rules is empty, every field resolves to the global defaults.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema reference for IDE validation support.

rules.description: Ordered list of cache rules. Order is significant: resolution is first-match-per-field, so place specific rules above broad ones. Defaults to an empty list, which makes every field resolve to the global cache: defaults.

cache_rule.description: One cache rule: a required glob pattern plus an optional subset of cache settings to apply when the pattern matches.

pattern.description: Glob pattern matched against the full cache key in the form {bucket}/{object_key} (no leading slash). Glob syntax: '*' matches any run of characters except '/' (one path segment), '**' matches any run including '/' (crosses segments), '?' matches exactly one character except '/', and every other character is a literal (regex metacharacters are escaped, so '.' matches only a literal '.'). Patterns are anchored to the whole key (they match only if they match the entire string) and matching is case-sensitive. A leading literal bucket name (e.g. "mybucket/temp/**") scopes a rule to one bucket; a leading wildcard (e.g. "**/logs/**") matches across all buckets; a wildcard before a literal segment (e.g. "**/credit-cards/**") matches that segment anywhere. Must be non-empty. Note: for access points, MRAPs, and S3-compatible hosts the cache key uses a different leading segment ({name}-{account}-s3alias, {alias}.mrap, or the bare normalized path) - see docs/CACHING.md.

get_ttl.description: TTL for cached GET responses. Human-readable duration: e.g. "0s", "30s", "5m", "1h", "7d". Set to "0s" to cache on disk but revalidate with S3 on every request (conditional GET with If-Modified-Since / If-None-Match). When "0s", range data is excluded from RAM cache (ram_cache_eligible is forced false).

head_ttl.description: TTL for cached HEAD responses. Same duration format as get_ttl. Set to "0s" to revalidate HEAD responses with S3 on every request.

put_ttl.description: TTL for write-cached PUT responses. Controls how long PUT data is retained on disk for subsequent GET requests.

read_cache_enabled.description: Enable or disable read caching for GET responses. When false, GET responses are streamed directly from S3 without caching to disk or RAM, and ram_cache_eligible is forced false. Write caching (PUT) is independent. Default: true (from global config).

write_cache_enabled.description: Enable or disable write-through caching for PUT operations. When false, PUT requests are forwarded to S3 but not cached locally. Default: from global config.

compression_enabled.description: Enable or disable LZ4 compression for cached data. Disable for keys containing already-compressed data (images, videos, archives). Default: from global config.

ram_cache_eligible.description: Whether range data for matching keys can be stored in RAM cache. Set to false to reserve RAM cache for higher-priority keys. Automatically forced to false when get_ttl is "0s" or read_cache_enabled is false. Default: true.

evaluate_conditions_from_cache.description: Evaluate client conditional headers (If-Match, If-None-Match, If-Modified-Since, If-Unmodified-Since) against cached metadata instead of forwarding to S3. When true, the proxy returns 304/412/200 locally from unexpired cache; when false (default), all conditional requests are forwarded to S3 for strict RFC 7232 semantics. Falls back to forward when cache metadata is expired or missing validators.
